title:
Lesson 1:
No Idea / Not Sure
description: 'Ni idea' = No idea (very casual). 'No tengo idea' = I have no idea. 'No estoy seguro/segura' = I'm not sure. Use 'seguro' if you're male, 'segura' if female.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: ¿Sabes a qué hora llega el tren?
dialogue.english: Do you know what time the train arrives?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: And you? Do you know anything?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: No estoy seguro. Creo que a las tres.
dialogue.english: I'm not sure. I think at three.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: No tengo idea tampoco. Voy a buscar en mi teléfono.
dialogue.english: I have no idea either. I'll look it up on my phone.

title:
Lesson 2:
Doubt & I Knew It
description: 'Lo sabía' = I knew it. 'Lo dudo' = I doubt it. 'No creo' = I don't think so. These express confidence or skepticism about information.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Dicen que va a llover mañana.
dialogue.english: They say it's going to rain tomorrow.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Lo dudo. Hoy está muy soleado.
dialogue.english: I doubt it. Today it's very sunny.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I don't think so. They're usually wrong.
